title: Digital Signatures with Ed25519 and ECDSA · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds versioned signed artifact verifier, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned signed artifact verifier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.
Generate, parse, sign, and verify Ed25519 keys and signatures with exact message/context rules and trusted key selection.
Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Signature Guarantees
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 2015 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Signature Guarantees.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Signature Guarantees, keep Crypto Go Signature Guarantees connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Signature Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-signature-policy-1m9s44" that uses case 8207 to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.
